title: Action Plan: Edinburgh Historical Weather via Open-Meteo (Filesystem JSON first)
goal: Fetch hourly historical weather for Edinburgh, UK from Open-Meteo and persist normalized JSON files to the local filesystem, with restart-safe incremental backfill.

must_be_confirmed_before_build: Usage mode confirmed: non-commercial prototype vs commercial production.
Open-Meteo plan tier confirmed for production (Standard/Professional/Enterprise as needed).
Exact historical date range to ingest first (for example full 5 years or specific start date).
Canonical timezone policy (UTC storage, display timezone handling).
Final hourly variable list and units.
Filesystem base path and retention policy for JSON outputs.
Run schedule (manual, cron, or on-demand only).
Error/retry policy and acceptable freshness lag.
Call-cost model validated (variables/time-window can count as fractional multiple calls).
credentials_and_access: For non-commercial prototype use, free/open-access endpoint can be used without API key (no SLA).
For commercial production use, customer API with API key is required per Open-Meteo pricing/licence terms.
Historical Weather API access for customer tiers must be confirmed for selected plan (Professional/Enterprise where applicable).
Outbound network access to the selected Open-Meteo endpoint/domain must be available in runtime environment.
definition_of_ready_checklist: Plan tier/API key available if production/commercial
Rate/call budget documented for chosen query shape

included: Filesystem JSON storage and metadata manifest
Read API endpoint for local JSON outputs

phases.name: Upfront Requirements Gate
tasks: Collect and lock all required inputs listed in upfront_requirements.
Validate Open-Meteo licensing fit for intended usage (commercial vs non-commercial).
If commercial/production: secure API key and customer endpoint before implementation.
Estimate monthly call volume based on variables, time-window length, models, and locations.
Record approved variables/date range/schedule in docs.
Confirm runtime network access to chosen Open-Meteo endpoint.
Do not start implementation until definition_of_ready_checklist is complete.

phases.name: Overview and Scope
tasks: Define canonical Edinburgh coordinates and timezone behavior.
Define hourly fields to ingest: temperature_2m, relativehumidity_2m, windspeed_10m, precipitation.
Define time range policy (initial backfill start/end and incremental cadence).
Define output structure: one JSON file per month plus run manifest.

phases.name: Filesystem Data Layer
tasks: Create weather storage directory (for example data/weather/open-meteo/edinburgh).
Define JSON schema for hourly records and monthly file envelope.
Create sync-state JSON file (cursor, last_successful_window, checksum/row counts).
Implement atomic write pattern (tmp file then rename) to avoid partial writes.

phases.name: Backend Integration Layer
tasks: Add Open-Meteo client with explicit URL builder and strict parameter validation.
Implement windowed downloader (monthly windows by default) with retry/backoff.
Normalize API payload into canonical local shape.
Merge-write strategy: dedupe by timestamp and overwrite only changed rows.
Add service orchestration methods: backfill(), resume(), incrementalUpdate().

phases.name: API and UX Layer
tasks: Add endpoint to trigger backfill/update for Edinburgh weather.
Add endpoint to query stored weather windows from JSON files.
Expose run status summary (current window, records written, errors).
Keep UX minimal: trigger plus latest status text.

phases.name: Testing
tasks: Unit tests for URL builder, normalization, and merge/dedupe logic.
Integration tests for windowed sync using mocked Open-Meteo responses and real filesystem writes.
Integration tests for resume-from-checkpoint after simulated interruption.
E2E test for trigger endpoint and status visibility.

phases.name: Documentation
tasks: Document Open-Meteo endpoint usage and parameter choices.
Document filesystem JSON schema and checkpoint format.
Document limitations: reanalysis source, no station SLA.

phases.name: Deployment and Verification
tasks: Run lint/typecheck/test gates before merge.
Run first historical backfill and verify monthly file counts.
Validate idempotent rerun produces no duplicate hourly rows.
Capture operational notes for future DB migration.

notes: Open-Meteo free/open-access is suitable for prototyping and non-commercial usage; production/commercial requires paid customer API.
Persist only normalized JSON now; DB migration comes later.
